desc: Dragon's Rest is a location on Stormwreck Isle
The crypts are part of Delzoun
Stormwreck Isle is part of the Sword Coast
The ruins are part of the Netherese Empire (implied through context)
Fighters dwell in the crypts of Delzoun
Rogues prowl the dark alleyways of Neverwinter
Rogues prowl the dark alleyways of Baldur's Gate
Clerics serve the gods
Monstrous beasts attack farms and freeholds
Dragons pose threats to settlements
Text references the adventure module associated with the location, but no explicit inhabitants are described
Three metallic dragons battled and imprisoned Sharruth beneath the ocean floor
Runara killed the blue dragon attempting to harness destructive magic
Sharruth was imprisoned beneath the island
Chromatic dragons claim Sharruth might still live and return to the island
Runara dwells at Dragon's Rest, devoted to healing the island's wounds
Mutual animosity between metallic and chromatic dragons stemming from Bahamut vs Tiamat
Runara resides in the cloister of Dragon's Rest
Appendix A contains the descriptions of magic items
The ship travels toward Stormwreck Isle
The party is traveling from Neverwinter to Stormwreck Isle
The monster moves onto the deck, indicating proximity to this location
These waters belong to the Scaled Queen, indicating ownership
The Merrow Extortionist demands tribute to the Scaled Queen, implying allegiance or servitude to her
Players attack the Merrow Extortionist during combat
Sailors are aboard the ship
The merrow disappears into the ocean deeps
Runara resides in Dragon's Rest
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest
The boat is adjacent to the cloister, allowing visibility from the water
The harbor is situated on the island
The temple is built on the island
The winding path ascends to the temple
The dock is within the harbor
The rowboat is secured to the dock
The zombies are encountered on the upward path
The beach is adjacent to the water's edge
Sacred Flame cantrip deals radiant damage to zombies, allowing it to bypass Undead Fortitude
The temple (area A5) is located within Dragon's Rest
Players evade combat with zombies
Monastic cells are part of the cloister
Dining room is part of the cloister
Temple is part of the cloister

Kobolds reside in the monastic cells
Myla operates within the cloister
The kobolds reside within the cloister
Humans observe the cloister from a distance
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest

The kobolds are located within the cloister
The elderly human woman is wearing a simple white robe
Runara hides in her secret underground lair

Tunnel opens to the open ocean
Temple at area A5 is part of Dragon's Rest
Cloister exists within Dragon's Rest complex
Runara oversees the cloister residents
Runara protects the cloister residents

Agga keeps the rest of the kobolds organized and in line
Agga keeps the rest of the kobolds organized and in line
Myla possesses the alchemical fire
Varnoth commanded the Azure Wolves
Varnoth resides at Dragon's Rest
Varnoth maintains the temple
The path begins at the rocky shore
Garden plots are positioned along the lower portion of the path
The path widens into the plaza above the bay
The stone statue is located within the plaza
The dragon statue depicts Astalagan, a bronze dragon

The lever is physically part of the winch

The building is situated halfway up the path
An iron chain is attached to the building

The cloister residents associate with the kitchen (implied by the context of sharing meals)
The long table is located within the dining room
The benches are located within the dining room
The chair is located within the dining room
The hallway directly connects to the kitchen
Runara spends nearly half her time in the library
Tarak comes to the library to read
Varnoth comes to the library to read
Myla visits the library and is described as studious
Many kobolds visit the library primarily for quiet
Open-air temple is part of the cloister
Temple is connected to the cliff face via carved walls
Pillars support the wooden roof
Statue is centrally located in the temple
Canaries perch on the statue
The residents of Dragon's Rest reside in the location
Runara interacts with the other residents of Dragon's Rest
Dragon's Rest is adjacent to Seagrow Caves
Dragon's Rest is adjacent to Compass Rose (shipwreck)

Exploration of Compass Rose (shipwreck) connects to the Clifftop Observatory
Zombies attack Blepp the kobold
Zombies attack Tarak the human gardener
Runara assigns a quest to investigate the shipwreck

Another villager resides in Dragon's Rest
Villager abandons fishing gear during escape
Second villager abandons fishing gear during escape
Undead sailors chase the fleeing villager
Undead sailors pursue the second villager
Zombies reside on the island
The fungal octopus protects the sea caves
The myconids dwell within the sea caves
The rocks north of Dragon's Rest are adjacent to the town
Runara directs adventurers to investigate the wreck of Compass Rose
Runara directs the character to investigate the wreck of Compass Rose after discussing the dream
The cloister is part of Dragon's Rest
The paladin comes to Dragon's Rest seeking rest
The paladin spends time in the temple of Bahamut
Runara welcomes the paladin at Dragon's Rest
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Tarak
Runara encourages the paladin to talk to Varnoth
Tarak was a member of the Gilded Gallows before betraying them
The lost fortune is alleged to be hidden on the island
Runara resides at Dragon's Rest
Runara provides the moonstone key to the characters
Aidron fled toward the ancient observatory
The ancient observatory is a second dragon's resting place near Dragon's Rest
Zombies were encountered at the shipwreck
Dragon's Rest is part of Stormwreck Isle
One of the adventure locations is part of Stormwreck Isle
Another adventure location is part of Stormwreck Isle
A third adventure location is part of Stormwreck Isle
The Fume Drake guardians protect the hot spring
The hot spring is situated on/in the island
Attempting to gather mushrooms provokes the Fume Drake guardians to attack
The brass dragon resided at the spring before its death
Wind spores are found in association with the spring
The owlbear resides in the northern rocks after being stranded there
The owlbear was formerly a member of the performing troupe
The whistle is attached to the owlbear's neck
The performing troupe originated from the island (implied by characters traveling across it)
The kobold renegades reject the peaceful teaching of Runara
The kobold renegades reject the tyrannical rule of Sparkrender
The kobold renegades attempt to ambush and attack travelers
The kobold renegades prey on travelers who stray away from Dragon's Rest
The kobolds attack the player (implied target not explicitly defined in text)
No explicit relationships defined in the text
Seagrow Caves are part of Stormwreck Isle
Sharruth is imprisoned beneath Stormwreck Isle
Fume drakes lurk in Seagrow Caves
Fire snakes lurk in Seagrow Caves
Kobolds serve Sharruth
Seagrow Caves are located on the southwest side of Stormwreck Isle
Myconids colonize the caves
Myconids give rare fungi to Tarak in exchange for waste
Blighted fumes from the tomb spill into the caves
Myconids placed a guardian to block access to their caves
Stirges pose a danger to intruders in the caves
Food scraps and waste from the cloister nourish the fungi
The basalt forming the cave walls originated from Sharruth's undersea tomb
Area B6 is a subsection of the Seagrow Caves
Bioluminescent fungi provide dim light throughout the caves
Toxic fumes cause a poisonous condition upon exposure
Lesser restoration spell ends the poison condition
Taking a long rest in the caves before opening B6's vent may trigger poisoning
Characters can take a rowboat from Dragon's Rest to reach Seagrow Caves.
The rowboat is available at Dragon's Rest.
The cliff is positioned above the cave mouth where waves enter and exit
The opening is situated in the cliff face
Flooding allows access from area B1 to area B2
Natural stairways descend from cliffs into the sea
Pathway follows base of cliffs during low tide
Pathway runs along edge of tunnel during low tide
The myconid's activity is tied to the cave environment
Communication effect extends beyond the cave boundary
Myconids in area B2 move to area B4 when detecting distress spores
Myconids in area B3 move to area B4 when detecting distress spores
The multicolored fungus is contained within the tunnel
The water is contained within the cavern
The colorful spores are suspended in the water
The stirges are clinging to the tunnel ceiling
A natural staircase leads from the cavern to the upper cave area
Water collects in the large pool within the upper cave area
Two mushroom-like people work near the large pool
The mushroom is located within the cave

An inactive violet fungus resides in the fungus farm
Another inactive violet fungus resides in the fungus farm
The stirge nest is physically located in the larder
One of the Myconid Sprouts resides in the larder
Another Myconid Sprout resides in the larder
The third Myconid Sprout resides in the larder
The larder is spatially adjacent to area B4
Six Stirge emerge from the nest and attack intruders
The mushroom-folk work amid the filth in the cave
The bulbous object clings to the wall and ceiling of the cave
Six clusters of giant mushrooms are arranged in a rough circle around this cavern
Several human-sized mushroom folk stand in a circle in the center of the cave
Auranta tends to Sinensa
Enok tends to Sinensa
Auranta collects spores from the glowing red fungus
Enok collects spores from the glowing red fungus
Auranta defends Sinensa
Enok defends Sinensa

Enok attacks intruders not accompanied by other myconids
Glowing fungus grows from the walls and ceilings of the cave
Colorful crystals grow from the walls and ceilings of the cave
A mushroom folk resides in the cave
Another mushroom folk resides in the cave
The large mushroom specimen resides in the cave
The ruby morel is brought to the cloister
The Fume Drake reside in the Crystal Cave
The tomb of Sharruth is located beneath the island
The tomb's presence causes volcanic activity producing fumes in the cave
The cave is the source of the blight spreading through the myconid colony
The orange crystal originates from the Elemental Plane of Fire
The glowing orange crystal is wedged into the fissure

Soot traces a path between the purple crystals and the fissure
The orange crystal blocks the western fissure
The orange crystal is found in the cave
The fire snake attacks the characters
The fire snake's egg breaks into chunks of obsidian
The third fume drake is added to the room when characters arrive
The fire snake is in the room
One fume drake emerges from the cluster of purple crystals
Another fume drake emerges from the cluster of purple crystals
Characters proceed to Seagrow Caves after exploring the Cursed Shipwreck
The wreck is located approximately 2½ miles from the cloister
The wreck is held up by ancient dragon bones that tore its hull
The hold is a portion of the wreck's interior space
Dragon's Rest possesses a rowboat for accessing Compass Rose
Main deck (area C1) is part of the Compass Rose wreck

The derelict ship is lodged against the ridge of rocks

Crow's nest is topped on the mainmast
